steps.title: Given equation
steps.content: We are given that 4x=3. We need to find the value of 24x. To do this, we can multiply both sides of the equation by a factor.

steps.title: Multiplying through
steps.content: To find 24x, notice that 24 is just 6 times 4. Therefore, we multiply both sides of the given equation by 6: \[6\cdot(4x) = 6\cdot3\] This simplifies to: $$24x=18$$

steps.title: Obtaining the final answer
steps.content: From this calculation, we see that the value of 24x is 18. That matches choice C — C is correct.
